description: Atlantis Golf Club is a member-owned private club with 27 holes of championship golf tucked inside the small city of Atlantis. Originally opened in 1960 and converted to a fully private club in 1972, the course underwent a full restoration of greens, tees, and bunkers in 2003. Membership is capped, and no advance tee times are required.
notes: Distinct from Atlantis Country Club. Private 27-hole member-owned facility. Sunbiz entity: Atlantis Golf Club, Inc.

description: Atlantis Country Club delivers a Dick Wilson and Robert Simmons-designed par-72 layout within the quiet residential city of Atlantis, just south of West Palm Beach. Founded in 1972, the semi-private club offers public tee times year-round alongside membership options. The course plays to over 6,500 yards and winds through a mature residential setting.
notes: Semi-private, public tee times available. 190 Atlantis Blvd.

description: The North Course at Quail Ridge Country Club is one of two Joe Lee-designed championship layouts within this 600-acre private community in Boynton Beach. Built in 1974, it plays as a traditional tree-lined Florida parkland design with generous fairways and well-bunkered greens. Membership is mandatory for residents of Quail Ridge.
notes: Private mandatory-membership community. North course opened 1974, South 1977.

description: Pine Tree Golf Club is a traditional private club in Boynton Beach, built in 1961 and set well apart from the surrounding development. The layout rewards precision over power and has maintained a classic member-focused atmosphere for over six decades. Considered one of the more understated and well-kept private clubs in Palm Beach County.
notes: Private, members and guests only. 10600 Pine Tree Terrace.

description: Hunters Run Country Club is a private 55+ community in Boynton Beach featuring 54 holes of championship golf spread across three courses. Founded in 1978, the club sits on a sprawling gated campus with extensive amenities and a mandatory membership structure for residents. The three courses vary in character and offer a full rotation for active golfers.
notes: 55+ community, mandatory membership, 54 holes. 3500 Clubhouse Ln.

description: The South Course at Quail Ridge Country Club complements the North layout within this Joe Lee-designed 36-hole complex, opening three years after its counterpart in 1977. Set within a 600-acre private gated community, it shares the North's emphasis on strategic tree-lined play and demands consistent ball-striking. Both courses are reserved exclusively for members and their guests.
notes: South course opened 1977. Private mandatory-membership community.

description: The Links at Boynton Beach is a public 27-hole facility designed by Bruce Devlin and Robert von Hagge, opened in 1984 and operated by the City of Boynton Beach. The complex includes a championship course and a shorter executive layout, making it accessible to golfers of all skill levels. It remains one of the busiest and most affordable public golf options in Palm Beach County.
notes: City-owned 27-hole public facility. Championship + executive courses.

description: Delray Dunes Golf and Country Club is a private Pete Dye-designed course in Boynton Beach, first opened in 1969 and recognized as one of his early Florida works. The layout winds through mature pines and palmettos with classic Dye-style bunkering and elevated greens. It operates as a private residential community club with mandatory membership.
notes: Pete Dye design, 1969. Private mandatory-membership community. 12005 Dunes Rd.

description: Palm Beach Leisureville Golf Course is an 18-hole all-par-3 layout within the Leisureville 55+ retirement community in Boynton Beach, opened in 1970. Playing to just over 1,800 yards, it serves as a walkable recreational amenity for community residents. Access is primarily limited to residents and their guests.
notes: All par-3, 18 holes, 55+ community. 1007 Ocean Dr, Boynton Beach. Semi-private for residents.

description: Westchester Golf and Country Club is a semi-private 18-hole facility in Boynton Beach built in 1989, offering public tee times alongside membership options. The course plays through a residential community with tree-lined fairways and has been a fixture of Palm Beach County public-access golf for over three decades. Affordable rates and convenient location make it a popular choice for local players.
notes: Semi-private, public tee times available. 12250 Westchester Club Dr.

description: Aberdeen Golf and Country Club is a private mandatory-membership residential community in Boynton Beach, built in 1987. The 18-hole course winds through the community's manicured landscape and serves as the centerpiece of an active country club lifestyle. Membership is required for all homeowners within the gated development.
notes: Mandatory membership, private community. 8251 Aberdeen Dr.

description: Medalist Golf Club in Hobe Sound is a co-design by Pete Dye and Greg Norman, opened in 1995, and best known as Tiger Woods's home course and the place where he has honed his game over the years. The layout demands precision and creativity through undulating terrain, with the exacting design standards both architects are known for. Membership is extremely limited and strictly by invitation.
notes: Tiger Woods home club. Co-design Pete Dye / Greg Norman. Ultra-private, invitation only.

description: Lost Lake Golf Club in Hobe Sound is a Tom Fazio-designed semi-private course opened in 1993, set within a residential community along the Treasure Coast. The layout offers a refined parkland experience with well-contoured greens and a welcoming membership structure that includes no waitlist. Daily fee play is available alongside membership options.
notes: Semi-private, public tee times and membership available. 8800 SE Fazio Dr.

description: Grove XXIII is Michael Jordan's ultra-private personal golf club in Hobe Sound, designed by Bobby Weed and opened in the fall of 2019. Named for Jordan's iconic number 23, the course is accessible only to Jordan and a very small circle of invited guests, making it among the most exclusive golf facilities in the country. The modernist clubhouse by Nichols Architects complements the minimalist design ethos of the layout.
notes: Michael Jordan's private club. Designed by Bobby Weed, opened 2019. Extremely exclusive, not open to any general membership.

description: Loblolly in Hobe Sound is a Pete and P.B. Dye-designed private course that opened in 1988, widely regarded as one of the Dye family's more quietly distinctive layouts in South Florida. The 275-member club was formed by merging the golf club with the Loblolly Bay Yacht Club and Marina, creating a unique dual-amenity private enclave. Membership is strictly by invitation.
notes: Pete and P.B. Dye design. Invitation-only private club. 7407 SE Hill Terrace.

description: McArthur Golf Club is an ultra-private facility in Hobe Sound designed for a select few members, built in 2002 on a property notable for its natural Florida landscape. The club keeps a very low profile with no public-facing booking and operates with an extremely limited membership. It sits within the broader Hobe Sound golf corridor alongside Medalist, Loblolly, and Grove XXIII.
notes: Ultra-private. 6550 SE Osprey St. Very limited information publicly available.

description: Hobe Sound Golf Club is a member-owned private club founded in 1988, designed by Tom Fazio and situated along the Treasure Coast in Martin County. The course occupies a naturally wooded site with native Florida vegetation framing its fairways, and the member-owned structure ensures a traditional country club atmosphere. No outside play is permitted.
notes: Member-owned, Tom Fazio design. 11671 SE Plandome Dr.

description: Seminole Golf Club in Juno Beach is one of the most celebrated private golf clubs in the United States, designed by Donald Ross in 1929 on a narrow strip of land between the Atlantic Ocean and the Intracoastal Waterway. Consistently ranked among America's top ten courses, it features Ross's hallmark contoured greens and a natural links character shaped by ocean breezes. Access is strictly limited to members and their guests, and the club has no website.
notes: Donald Ross 1929. One of the top private clubs in America. No outside play whatsoever. 901 Seminole Blvd.

description: The Bears Club in Jupiter is a Jack Nicklaus Signature design founded in 1999 by Jack and Barbara Nicklaus, with membership capped at approximately 100 families to preserve an intimate private atmosphere. The par-72 layout plays through a lushly landscaped residential community and reflects Nicklaus's signature strategic demands with large, undulating greens. It is one of the most exclusive private clubs in South Florida.
notes: Jack Nicklaus Signature design, ~100 member cap. 250 Bears Club Drive.

description: Abacoa Golf Club in Jupiter is one of South Florida's well-regarded public championship courses, designed by Joe Lee and opened in October 1999 within the Abacoa planned community. The 18-hole layout plays over 6,700 yards and winds through the community's neighborhoods, offering daily fee golf with no membership required. It hosts regular tournaments and is managed by the Abacoa Property Owners Association.
notes: Public championship course. 105 Barbados Dr.

description: Jonathan's Landing Golf Club is a premier private golf, tennis, and boating community in Jupiter, founded in 1985 along the Intracoastal Waterway. The club features multiple courses including designs by Tom Fazio, offering members a diverse rotation of championship golf within a waterfront residential enclave. A new Beau Welling Design course was added in 2024, keeping the facility at the forefront of modern course design.
notes: Multiple courses including Fazio and new Beau Welling (2024). Private. 16823 Captain Kirle Dr.

description: Jupiter Dunes Golf Club is a public 18-hole executive course situated along the A1A corridor in Jupiter, designed by Thomas Fazio and opened in 1973. The compact layout plays just over 3,000 yards and sits near the beach, providing an accessible and scenic option for golfers seeking a shorter game. It operates as a daily fee facility with a full practice facility.
notes: Public executive course. 401 N A1A, Jupiter. Thomas Fazio design.

description: Jupiter Country Club is a private residential country club community in Jupiter, anchored by a Greg Norman-designed 18-hole championship course that opened in 2007. The club is managed by Invited (formerly Club Corp) and offers a full amenity package within its gated residential development. Membership is tied to the community's residential program.
notes: Greg Norman design, 2007. Managed by Invited. Private residential community.

description: Trump National Golf Club Jupiter is a private 18-hole course built in 2002 and acquired by Donald Trump, located along the Loxahatchee River in Jupiter. The course originally opened as The Reserve and was redesigned after Trump's purchase, offering a demanding layout through a gated residential community. Membership is available by application and the club hosts limited private events.
notes: Private. Formerly The Reserve. 115 Eagle Tree Ter, Jupiter.

description: The Dye Preserve in Jupiter is a Pete Dye-designed private retreat opened in 2002 on land that originally housed Cypress Links in the late 1980s. Dye's layout weaves through a preserved natural setting with his trademark railroad ties, forced carries, and contoured greens demanding shotmaking creativity. Guest access is available through member invitations and occasional club-hosted arrangements.
notes: Pete Dye design 2002. Site originally opened as Cypress Links 1988. 1808 SE Colony Way.

description: The Golf Club of Jupiter is a locally owned and operated public course in Jupiter, offering 18 holes of accessible daily fee golf since 1981. The layout provides a traditional Florida design with Bermuda fairways and is one of the more affordable public options in the northern Palm Beach County area. The club maintains a relaxed atmosphere focused on the everyday golfer.
notes: Public, locally owned. 1800 S Central Blvd. Some sources cite founding history to 1940, current course 1981.

description: Fountains Country Club in Lake Worth is a private member-owned community established in 1972, featuring three 18-hole courses — North, South, and West — set within a 965-acre gated development of 1,767 homes. The courses provide a variety of layouts suited to different skill levels, all maintained to a high private-club standard. Membership is tied to residence in the community.
notes: 36+ holes, private, 1,767-home community. 4476 Fountains Dr.

description: Park Ridge Golf Course is a Palm Beach County-operated public 18-hole course in Lake Worth, built in 2007 on a reclaimed landfill site — a rarity in Florida golf development. The course opened in March 2007 and is maintained by the county's Parks and Recreation department, offering affordable access to residents. The site's former use required creative design solutions that resulted in distinctive elevation changes unusual for South Florida.
notes: Built on reclaimed landfill. County-operated public course. 9191 Lantana Rd.

description: Forest Oaks Golf Club is a public 18-hole course in Lake Worth that has been serving Palm Beach County golfers since 1971. The layout is a traditional Florida design with tree-lined fairways and is operated as a daily fee facility accessible to all. It represents one of the longer-tenured public golf options in the area.
notes: Public daily fee. 144 Lucerne Lakes Blvd.

description: Winston Trails Golf Club in Lake Worth is a semi-private 18-hole facility built in 1993, offering public tee times alongside a membership program within a residential community. The course was designed with water features characteristic of South Florida golf and provides a consistent challenge for a broad range of handicap levels. It is one of the more accessible semi-private options in the Lake Worth corridor.
notes: Semi-private. 6101 Winston Trails Blvd.

description: Lake Worth Municipal Golf Course — now operating as Lake Worth Beach Golf Club — is one of the oldest public courses in Florida, having been in play since 1927. The city-owned 18-hole layout sits at 1 7th Ave North and offers affordable public golf in the heart of the Lake Worth Beach community. Its long history makes it a historic fixture of public golf in Palm Beach County.
notes: City-owned municipal. Also known as Lake Worth Beach Golf Club. 1 7th Ave N.

description: Palm Beach National Golf and Country Club in Lake Worth is a public 18-hole course built in 1962, offering daily fee golf on a mature Florida layout along Saint Andrews Road. The course has been a Palm Beach County staple for over sixty years and features a traditional design with tree-lined fairways and Bermuda greens. It is a semi-private club with public tee times widely available.
notes: Public tee times widely available. 7500 Saint Andrews Rd.

description: The Falls Club of the Palm Beaches is a private 18-hole club in Lake Worth designed by Joe Lee and opened in 1985 on 170 acres. The layout has earned a reputation as one of the better membership values in South Florida private golf, with a course that challenges without being punishing. It operates as a private facility for members and their guests.
notes: Private, Joe Lee design. 6455 Jog Rd.

description: Poinciana Country Club in Lake Worth is a semi-private executive par-64 course designed by Leon Howard and opened in 1976, set within the Poinciana 55+ community. The layout plays to 4,560 yards with a mix of par-4s, par-5s, and eleven par-3s, making it well-suited to the community's active adult residents. Public tee times are available alongside community membership.
notes: Executive par-64 course. 55+ community. 3536 Poinciana Dr.

description: North Palm Beach Country Club Golf Course is a village-owned public facility redesigned as a Jack Nicklaus Signature course and reopened for play in 2006 following an extensive renovation. Originally established in 1963, the course is open to the general public seven days a week and offers one of the few municipally owned Nicklaus-designed layouts in Florida. It sits at the heart of the Village of North Palm Beach.
notes: Village-owned public. Jack Nicklaus Signature redesign opened 2006. 951 US Hwy 1.

description: Lost Tree Club is an ultra-private golf club within the 450-acre Lost Tree Village community in North Palm Beach, built in 1961 on land stretching from the Atlantic Ocean to the Intracoastal Waterway. The course is the centerpiece of one of Palm Beach County's most secluded and exclusive residential enclaves. Access is strictly limited to the small community of members.
notes: Ultra-private. 11520 Lost Tree Way. Community of ~450 acres, ocean to Intracoastal.

description: The Everglades Club on Palm Beach island is one of the oldest and most exclusive private clubs in America, founded in 1919 and home to a historic nine-hole golf course added in 1920. The club's Worth Avenue address places it at the center of Palm Beach social life, and its membership is among the most rarefied in the country. The golf course is purely a member amenity within a broader social and dining club.
notes: Founded 1919 by Paris Singer and Addison Mizner. Private social club with golf. 356 Worth Ave.

description: Palm Beach Country Club is a Donald Ross-designed private course built in 1917 on a narrow 83-acre strip of Palm Beach island, stretching from the Atlantic Ocean to Lake Worth. One of the oldest clubs in Florida, it has maintained an understated, member-focused identity for over a century while consistently ranking among the region's most prestigious. Membership is by invitation only.
notes: Palm Beach Country Club — listed as 'Palm Beach Club' in input. Donald Ross 1917. 760 N Ocean Blvd.

description: The Ocean Course at The Breakers is Florida's oldest 18-hole golf course, first laid out in 1896 alongside Henry Flagler's iconic Palm Beach resort. The layout was redesigned by Brian Silva in 2000 and again renovated by Rees Jones, and it retains a classic parkland character with palm-lined corridors and water hazards. Access is restricted to resort guests and Breakers Club members.
notes: Florida's oldest 18-hole course. Resort/club access only — hotel guests and members. 1 S County Rd.

description: Palm Beach Par 3 Golf Course is a town-owned oceanside par-3 layout on the island of Palm Beach, originally designed by Dick Wilson and Joe Lee and opened in 1961. Situated between the Atlantic Ocean and the Intracoastal Waterway on South Ocean Boulevard, it was redesigned in 2009 by Raymond Floyd and has twice been named the best par-3 course in the country by Golf Digest. It is open to the public and remains one of the most scenic golf experiences in Florida.
notes: Town of Palm Beach owned. Dick Wilson/Joe Lee 1961, Raymond Floyd redesign 2009. 2345 S Ocean Blvd.

description: PGA The Estates course at PGA National is a Karl Litten-designed layout that originally opened in November 1984 as Stonewall Golf Club before being absorbed into the PGA National campus in 1988. It is part of the PGA National Members Club and is situated within a gated residential area of the broader PGA National community. Access is tied to membership at PGA National Members Club or resort stay.
notes: Part of PGA National campus. Originally Stonewall Golf Club, opened 1984. Karl Litten architect. City listed as 'Palm Beach County' in input — correct city is Palm Beach Gardens.

description: Kings Point Par 3 is an 18-hole all-par-3 course within the Kings Point 55+ retirement community in Delray Beach, designed by Robert Trent Jones Sr. and opened in 1974. The course plays to approximately 2,100 yards and serves as a walkable recreational amenity for the community's residents. Access is primarily for Kings Point residents and their guests.
notes: 55+ community course. City input listed as 'Palm Beach County' — correct city is Delray Beach, FL 33446.

description: Boca Delray Golf and Country Club is a non-equity 55+ community club in Delray Beach, featuring an executive-length 18-hole course built around 1984. The club operates without mandatory membership and maintains a welcoming atmosphere for active adult residents and their guests. Public play is limited, with access primarily tied to the community's residential program.
notes: 55+ non-equity community. City input 'Palm Beach County' — correct city is Delray Beach. 5483 Boca Delray Blvd.

description: Kings Point Executive Golf Course is an 18-hole executive layout within the Kings Point 55+ community in Delray Beach, designed by Robert Trent Jones Sr. and opened in 1973. The course offers a longer and more challenging alternative to the community's par-3 layout, playing at executive length through the community's landscaped grounds. Access is reserved primarily for Kings Point residents.
notes: 55+ community executive course. City input 'Palm Beach County' — correct city is Delray Beach. 6561 Flanders Way.

description: Bocaire Country Club is a private member-owned community in Boca Raton built in 1984, featuring a single 18-hole championship course set within a gated residential enclave. The club is known for its attentive service and manicured playing conditions, and membership is mandatory for community residents. Its location in northern Boca Raton places it squarely within the Palm Beach County golf corridor.
notes: City input 'Palm Beach County' — correct city is Boca Raton. Private mandatory-membership. 4989 Bocaire Blvd.

description: Eastpointe Country Club in Palm Beach Gardens is a private 36-hole Fazio-designed community club developed in the early 1970s and opened in 1974. The two championship courses wind through a lush residential setting and are exclusively available to club members. An optional rather than mandatory membership structure distinguishes it from many of the area's mandatory-fee communities.
notes: City input 'Palm Beach County' — correct city is Palm Beach Gardens. Optional membership, two Fazio courses. 13535 Eastpointe Blvd.

description: BallenIsles Country Club in Palm Beach Gardens traces its origins to 1963 when John D. MacArthur and the PGA of America commissioned Dick Wilson and Joe Lee to design its first course — originally the PGA National Golf Club. Renamed BallenIsles in 1989 after operating as JDM Country Club, the club now offers three championship courses and a full amenity package within a private gated community. It stands as one of the most established private clubs in the Palm Beach Gardens area.
notes: Originally PGA National GC (1963), then JDM CC, renamed BallenIsles 1989. Three championship courses. 100 BallenIsles Cir.

description: The Nest Par 3 is a city-owned public par-3 course at Sandhill Crane Golf Club in Palm Beach Gardens, debuting in August 2023 as part of the city's Signature Facility initiative. The layout spans 3,100 yards from the tips with holes up to 256 yards and includes a 30,000-square-foot putting green and a distinctive island-green 19th hole. It is one of the most ambitious new public par-3 facilities in South Florida.
notes: Opened August 2023. City of Palm Beach Gardens owned. 9900 Sandhill Crane Drive.

description: The Fazio Course at PGA National Resort is the original course opened at the resort on March 4, 1980, later renamed The Fazio following a comprehensive redesign by Tom Fazio completed in November 2012. It plays through the resort's manicured landscape as one of five courses available to resort guests and PGA National Members Club members. The redesign modernized the layout while preserving its place as the resort's welcoming entry-level course.
notes: Original course at PGA National, opened 1980. Renamed The Fazio after Tom Fazio redesign 2012. Resort/member access. 400 Ave of Champions.

description: Frenchman's Creek Beach and Country Club is a private 36-hole community club in Palm Beach Gardens, with the South course dating to 1973 and set within 700 acres along Florida's Atlantic coast. The club's dual identity as both a golf and beach club distinguishes it among South Florida private clubs, giving members access to oceanfront amenities alongside championship golf. Membership is tied to the residential community.
notes: Private. 36 holes. Beach club access. 13495 Tournament Drive.

description: The Palmer Course at PGA National Resort was dedicated on February 28, 1984, and named in honor of Arnold Palmer. It gained widespread recognition as the longtime host of the Honda Classic — now the Cognizant Classic — on the PGA Tour, placing it among the most prominent tournament venues in Florida. Resort guests and PGA National Members Club members can access the course year-round.
notes: Host of Honda Classic / Cognizant Classic PGA Tour event. Opened 1984. Resort/member access. 400 Ave of Champions.

description: Frenchman's Reserve Country Club in Palm Beach Gardens is a private Arnold Palmer Design Company course developed by Toll Brothers and opened in 2016. The layout winds through a gated luxury residential community and reflects Palmer Design's signature blend of playability and strategic challenge. The club offers a full amenity package within the community for its members.
notes: Arnold Palmer Design, Toll Brothers development. 3370 Grande Corniche.

description: Sandhill Crane Golf Club is a city-operated public 18-hole course in Palm Beach Gardens, built in 1992 and managed by the City of Palm Beach Gardens. The championship layout is joined on campus by the newer Nest Par 3, making the Sandhill Crane complex one of the most complete public golf facilities in Palm Beach County. Daily fee play is available with no membership required.
notes: City of Palm Beach Gardens operated. 9500 Sandhill Crane Drive.

description: The Squire Course at PGA National Resort was built in 1981 and designed by Tom and George Fazio as part of the resort's original course lineup. It serves as one of the more player-friendly options within the PGA National portfolio, accessible to resort guests and PGA National Members Club members. A recent re-imagination project by Staples, ASGCA has updated the layout for modern play.
notes: Tom and George Fazio design, 1981. Recent redesign by Staples, ASGCA. Resort/member access.

description: PGA National Resort in Palm Beach Gardens is anchored by five championship courses — the Champion, Palmer, Fazio, Squire, and Estates — set within a 2,340-acre community that serves as the home of the PGA of America. The Champion Course is the resort's flagship, hosting the Cognizant Classic (formerly Honda Classic) on the PGA Tour each year. Golf is available to resort hotel guests and PGA National Members Club members across all courses.
notes: Multi-course resort. Access for hotel guests and members. Champion Course hosts Cognizant Classic. 400 Ave of Champions.

description: Old Palm Golf Club in Palm Beach Gardens is a private residential golf community founded in 2004, built around a Raymond Floyd design that navigates through a landscape of mature palms and native Florida vegetation. The club limits its membership to preserve an intimate atmosphere and offers a full-service amenity experience within the gated community. No outside play is permitted.
notes: Raymond Floyd design. Private residential community. 11889 Old Palm Dr.

description: Panther National is an ultra-luxury new private golf community in Palm Beach Gardens, designed by Jack Nicklaus and Justin Thomas and officially opened in November 2023 — the first new private golf community in Palm Beach County in nearly two decades. The course incorporates bold design elements with dramatic contouring, a modern clubhouse aesthetic, and a limited membership aimed at a new generation of private golf members. It represents the most significant new private golf development in South Florida in recent years.
notes: Opened November 2023. Jack Nicklaus + Justin Thomas co-design. Ultra-private, new development. 10991 Panther National Blvd.

description: The Country Club at Mirasol in Palm Beach Gardens is a private 36-hole community featuring courses designed by Tom Fazio and Arthur Hills, developed between 2001 and 2013 within a gated enclave of 1,164 single-family homes. The Fazio layout is widely regarded as the signature course of the community, playing through expansive water features and sculpted terrain. Membership is tied to residence and is managed as a full-service private club.
notes: Tom Fazio + Arthur Hills designs. 36 holes. Private residential. 11642 Mirasol Way.

description: Trump International Golf Club in West Palm Beach is a private 27-hole championship facility designed by Jim Fazio and built in 1999, located along Summit Boulevard adjacent to Forest Hill Boulevard. The club operates as a private membership facility with a reputation for immaculate conditioning and a high-end amenity package. It is one of two Trump-branded golf properties in the Palm Beach area.
notes: City input 'Palm Springs' — correct city is West Palm Beach. Jim Fazio design, 27 holes. 3505 Summit Blvd.

description: Tequesta Country Club is a private member-owned club in the Village of Tequesta, founded in 1957 by developer Charles Martyn alongside much of the surrounding residential community. The 18-hole layout sits along the Loxahatchee River and has served as a cornerstone of Tequesta social life for nearly seventy years. Membership is by application and the club maintains a traditional country club atmosphere.
notes: Founded 1957 by Charles Martyn. Private. 201 Country Club Dr.

description: Riverbend Golf Club in Tequesta is a private 18-hole course designed by Tom Fazio and founded in 1973, set along the border of Jonathan Dickinson State Park with the Loxahatchee River as a backdrop. The natural setting provides a distinctive character, with preserved Florida landscape framing the fairways throughout. Membership is by invitation and the club maintains a low-profile private atmosphere.
notes: Tom Fazio design. Borders Jonathan Dickinson State Park. 9300 SE Riverfront Terrace.

description: Turtle Creek Club in Tequesta is a private golf and country club founded in 1969, consistently recognized as one of the finest private clubs on Florida's Treasure Coast. The 18-hole layout is set within a mature, lushly landscaped environment along the Loxahatchee River, offering a refined private experience that has attracted a distinguished membership over five decades. Access is by member invitation only.
notes: Private. Founded 1969. 2 Club Circle, Tequesta.

description: The Country Club of Florida in the Village of Golf is an invitation-only private club founded in 1956, one year before the Village of Golf itself was incorporated by the Florida legislature. The 18-hole course occupies a compact footprint on Country Road within this tiny municipality — one of the smallest incorporated places in Florida — and has maintained an exclusive membership for nearly seventy years.
notes: Founded 1956, predates Village of Golf incorporation (1957). Invitation-only private. 21 Country Road.

description: Wycliffe Golf and Country Club in Wellington is a private 36-hole club established in 1989, offering two championship courses within a gated community of 1,400-plus homes. The East and West courses underwent significant renovations in 2015 and 2023 respectively, keeping the facility current while preserving its long-standing private club traditions. Membership is mandatory for community residents.
notes: Private, 36 holes. East opened 2015, West opened 2023. Mandatory membership. 4650 Wycliffe Country Club Blvd.

description: Binks Forest Golf Club in Wellington opened in 1990 to considerable acclaim, designed by Johnny Miller and Gene Bates and nominated as a Top 75 Golf Course by Golf Digest in 1996. The course has operated under various ownership structures including a closure and successful reopening in 2007 as Wellington National Golf Club. The layout features pine-lined fairways and a private-club aesthetic that distinguishes it from typical South Florida golf.
notes: Now operating as Wellington National Golf Club. Johnny Miller / Gene Bates design. 400 Binks Forest Dr.

description: The Wanderers Club in Wellington is a private golf and polo club established in 1984, making it one of the earliest private clubs in what would become the equestrian capital of the world. The 18-hole golf course complements the club's polo fields and equestrian facilities, creating a unique dual-sport private community. The combination of golf and polo in one club is rare in the United States.
notes: Private golf and polo club. 1900 Aero Club Dr.

description: Banyan Golf Club in West Palm Beach is a public 18-hole course designed by Joe Lee and established in 1973, offering affordable daily fee golf on a traditional Florida layout. The course is named for the area's distinctive banyan trees and has provided an accessible public golf option in western Palm Beach County for over fifty years. No membership is required.
notes: Public daily fee. 1393 Lyons Rd.

description: The Breakers Rees Jones Course at Breakers West is a resort-affiliated 18-hole course in West Palm Beach, originally built in 1968 and extensively renovated by Rees Jones, reopening in December 2022. It sits within the Breakers West residential community and is available to Breakers Club members and resort packages. Rees Jones's redesign modernized the layout with rebuilt tees, new bunkering, and salt-tolerant turf throughout.
notes: Originally built 1968, Rees Jones renovation reopened December 2022. Resort/member access. 1550 Flagler Pkwy.

description: Mayacoo Lakes Country Club in West Palm Beach is a private 18-hole club built in 1972, situated in western Palm Beach County along a series of interconnected lakes that define the character of the layout. The course rewards controlled ball-striking and course management given the persistent water hazards, and has maintained a stable private membership for over five decades.
notes: Private. 9697 Mayacoo Club Dr.

description: Okeeheelee Golf Course is a Palm Beach County-operated public 27-hole facility in West Palm Beach, built in 1995 within the expansive Okeeheelee Park. The course offers three nine-hole combinations across a quiet park setting with minimal residential intrusion, making it one of the more tranquil public golf experiences in the county. Daily fee rates make it accessible to all levels of golfer.
notes: County-operated, 27 holes. 7715 Forest Hill Blvd.

description: Bear Lakes Country Club in West Palm Beach is a private 36-hole facility established in 1984, featuring the Lakes Course and the Links Course — the latter a Jack Nicklaus design. The club operates as a traditional private membership club with a focus on competitive golf, hosting FSGA events and junior programs. The two contrasting course designs give members a diverse playing experience.
notes: Private, 36 holes. Links Course is Nicklaus Design. 1901 Village Blvd.

description: The Preserve at Ironhorse is a small private golf club in West Palm Beach built in 1989, designed by Arthur Hills and later refreshed by Rees Jones. The course weaves through a residential community in western Palm Beach County and maintains a focused private club experience with tennis, pool, and dining amenities alongside the 18-hole layout.
notes: Arthur Hills original design, Rees Jones renovation. Private. 8055 Ironhorse Blvd.

Could not verify a golf course named 'Long Pine Country Club' in West Palm Beach, FL. No records found under this name. Possible data entry error — may refer to Lone Pine Golf Club (closed) at 6251 N Military Trl, WPB 33407, which was a public executive course built 1978/1981. Recommend verifying course name.

description: The Park West Palm is a new-era public golf destination in West Palm Beach, opened in March 2023 with Tiger Woods attending the grand opening. The facility features a championship 18-hole course alongside the Lit 9 par-3 course, a large practice facility, and premium food and beverage operations, targeting both serious golfers and a broader entertainment audience. It is one of the most significant new public golf investments in South Florida in decades.
notes: Opened March 2023. Tiger Woods grand opening. 7301 Georgia Ave. Championship course + 9-hole Lit 9 + entertainment.

description: The Club at Ibis in West Palm Beach is a private residential community featuring three Nicklaus family-designed courses — the Legend (Jack Nicklaus), the Heritage (Jack Nicklaus II), and the Tradition (Steve Nicklaus) — making it the only private country club in the world with three Nicklaus-family course designs. The Legend Course opened in 1991 with Jack Nicklaus present for the ceremonial first shot and is considered his masterpiece in Palm Beach County. The club offers a full resort-style amenity package within the gated community.
notes: Three Nicklaus-family courses. Legend opened 1991. Private. 8225 Ibis Blvd.

description: Madison Green Golf Club is a public 18-hole course in West Palm Beach built in 2001, operated as a daily fee facility within the Madison Green residential community. The course offers accessible golf for all skill levels with no membership required, and remains one of the newer public options in western Palm Beach County. Tee times are available online year-round.
notes: Public daily fee. 2001 Crestwood Blvd N.

description: Banyan Cay in West Palm Beach is a Jack Nicklaus-designed course that traces its origins to the President Country Club, originally built in 1969 and redeveloped with a full Nicklaus redesign completed in 2017. The property underwent foreclosure and ownership changes before reopening in late 2024 under a new brand as a luxury resort golf destination. The Nicklaus design features dramatic water features and a bold visual aesthetic suited to the resort context.
notes: Originally President Country Club (1969). Jack Nicklaus redesign 2017. Rebranded as Dutchman's Pipe / The Belgrove in late 2024. 3200 N Congress Ave.
